ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: I believe suicide prevention is an important part of my professional role
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I have the knowledge and training needed to recognize when a patient may be elevated risk for suicide
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I am knowledgeable about warning signs for suicide
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I am comfortable asking patients direct and open questions about suicidal thoughts and behaviors
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I have the knowledge and skills needed to screen patients for suicide risks
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I am comfortable screening patients for suicide risk
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I have the knowledge and skills needed to conduct a suicide risk assessment
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I am knowledgeable about risk factors for suicide
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I obtain information about risk and protective factors when conducting suicide risk assessments
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I assess the patient's access to lethal means as part of a suicide risk assessment
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I assess the patient's suicide plans and intentions as a part of a suicide risk assessment
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I am confident in my ability to conduct a suicide risk assessment

ElementDescription: I have the knowledge and skills needed to provide care to patients who have been identified as being at elevate risk for suicide
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I am comfortable providing care to patients who have been identified as being at elevated risk for suicide
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: I am confident in my ability to motivate patients to engage in treatment

ElementDescription: I am confident in my ability to work collaboratively with a patient to develop a safety plan that outlines specific steps if they experience suicidal thoughts or triggers
Notes: 0 = Strongly Disagree; 1 = Disagree; 2 = Neutral; 3 = Agree; 4 = Strongly Agree

ElementDescription: Help people to minimize the severity of risk to self
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Use specific interventions focusing on risks of harm to (or neglect of) others
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Help people to minimize the severity of risk to others
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Develop rapport with people who present with significant risks
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Manage risks in line with organizational confidentiality policies
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Use strategies to avoid malpractice liability or disciplinary action
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Develop a formal or written risk management plan
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Appropriately judge whether or not a person should be referred to an external service or professional on the basis of risk
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Identify an appropriate service to refer someone on the basis of risk
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Successfully refer and engage a person with an appropriate service
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Motivate a person to successfully self-refer to an appropriate service
Notes: 0=Not at all confident; 10=Highly confident

ElementDescription: Total ramses score (1-18)
Use screening instruments to assess risk

ElementDescription: Interview people to elicit key information about risk factors

ElementDescription: Identify a person who is presenting risk to self

ElementDescription: Identify a person who is presenting risk to others
